reviews.review_msg: This was my third class with Antone and I was once again NOT disappointed. His lectures and discussions are very informative and engaging. You can tell he really enjoys what he is teaching. He gives great feedback and is a very fair marker. Super caring prof, definitely recommend!

Ronda made Shakespeare enjoyable. She picked a great selection of plays and focused on the texts themselves, rather than critical theory. This lecture-style made the task of talking about Shakespeare less daunting. Her papers were typical of the third year level. She gives you a choice on what to write on. Not a difficult marker, just picky.

This was one of my favourite classes I ever took. Ronda was such a good lecturer and fantastic at creating student discussion. She came well prepared with engaging lecture content and was always very clear in her expectations. She answers emails fast and encouraged people to come to office hours, where she gave very good feedback on papers.

This is my last semester and 4th time taking a class with Torsten. I cannot stress enough that if there is an opportunity to take a class with him, you should take it. Without a doubt my favourite prof at SFU. He is kind, helpful, and understands that students lives are busy. He is very accommodating and passionate about what he teaches.
Such a great professor I've enrolled in his class 3 times...great class to take to gain credits with ease..he made my 4 course load feel like 3.

One of the most easy-going, helpful, engaging profs I've ever had. He knows his stuff, and he actually cares that his students learn it, too. He is beyond reasonable, understanding, and witty. Anyone who says otherwise is just afraid to be out-classed. Attend just ONE day--just the first class, please, and judge for yourself.
